title: Jelly's Sichuan Noodles
summary: This recipe is inspired by one of my favourites, Dan Dan Noodles. It’s got everything. It’s savoury, spicy, acidic and so addictive.

ingredients.label: 12 Tbsp Chilli Oil (We Love Lao Gan Ma)
ingredients.name: Chilli Oil

method.description: Place a wok over a medium high heat, tip in the Sichuan peppercorns, then toast for 2 mins until fragrant. Tip the toasted peppercorns into a mini chopper and blitz into a powder. If you don’t have a mini chopper or spice grinder, use a pestle and mortar to crush or finely chop with a knife. Set aside.
Slice your cucumber into matchsticks and put into a bowl of iced water and set aside. This is optional, but ensures that your cucumber stays crunchy.

Heat 2 tbsp of oil in the wok over a high heat, chuck in your pork mince and stir fry for 2-3 mins, until browned. Add the Shaoxing wine, 2 tbsp of the soy sauce, 1 tsp of the blitzed/crushed Sichuan peppercorns and 1 tsp sugar. Cook for a minute and then remove from the heat and set aside.
Drain the cucumber matchsticks and pat them dry. Lightly crush or roughly chop your peanuts. Bring a large pan of water to the boil. Season with salt.

Lay out 4 bowls and into each bowl add 1 tsp tahini, 3 tbsp chili oil, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp crushed Sichuan peppercorns, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp black vinegar, 1 tsp of MSG. Finely grate a garlic clove into each bowl. Do not mix it yet. This is going to create a delicious sauce.
Drop the noodles into the pan of boiling water and cook according to packet instructions. Remove from the heat and use a small jug to reserve a cup full of the noodle water. Drain the noodles.
Add around 3 tbsp of the noodle water to each bowl.

Assembly time. Place the hot noodles into each bowl along with a spoonful of the pork mince. Top with the cucumber and crushed peanuts. Just before eating, give a big mix so the sauce is coating the noodles. Eat straight away!

chefs.name: Angelica Udenweze
chefs.summary: Angelica Udenweze joined the team as a food assistant and helped develop so many classic dishes, during her time at Mob. When it comes to her cooking, Angelica specialises in complex flavour combos that always leave you wanting more.
